entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A widely cultivated cereal crop belonging to the Poaceae family.

entities.name: POACEAE FAMILY
entities.description: The grass family to which sorghum belongs.

entities.name: SORGHUM BICOLOR
entities.description: A species of sorghum cultivated for grain and forage.

entities.name: JOHNSON GRASS
entities.description: Sorghum halepense, a species cultivated for forage.

entities.name: SORGHUM PROPINQUUM
entities.description: A sorghum species cultivated for forage.

entities.name: COMMON WILD SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor subsp. verticilliflorum, a wild relative of sorghum.

entities.name: GRAIN
entities.description: A product for which Sorghum bicolor is cultivated.

entities.name: FORAGE
entities.description: A product for which Sorghum bicolor, Johnson grass, and Sorghum propinquum are cultivated.

entities.name: SORGHUM SYRUP
entities.description: A product produced from sweet sorghum.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to withstand dry conditions.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID AND DRY AREAS
entities.description: Environmental conditions where drought-tolerant sorghum is an excellent choice.

entities.name: ANNUAL LIFE CYCLE
entities.description: A growth habit where the plant completes its life cycle in one year.

entities.name: PERENNIAL LIFE CYCLE
entities.description: A growth habit where the plant lives for more than two years.

entities.name: SELF-POLLINATION
entities.description: The primary pollination method of sorghum.

entities.name: PROTOGYNY
entities.description: A condition where female organs mature before male organs, leading to cross-pollination.

entities.name: CROSS-POLLINATION
entities.description: Natural pollination between different plants, occurring in sorghum due to protogyny.

entities.name: SELFING
entities.description: The process of self-pollination used to maintain genetic integrity of sorghum accessions.
